description: Single machine-readable source of truth for the Skill-23 forbidden client-facing jargon list. INSTRUCTIONS.md and build-workforce.py are POINTERS to this file — do NOT define a second copy of this list anywhere else. The QC gate (scripts/qc-interview-completion.py) loads this file at runtime and scans AI-authored transcript text for any term listed here. PRD-2.15.

terms.rationale: Lean Six Sigma internal terminology that sounds bureaucratic to owners
Process-engineering jargon that does not resonate with business owners
Developer jargon that alienates non-technical owners
OpenClaw implementation detail — owners think in terms of people, not agents
Implementation detail that reveals system internals to the client
The word 'agent' in the context of AI automation is confusing or off-putting to non-technical owners; use role-based language instead
terms.clientAnswerNote: A client may say 'real estate agent' or 'I have a great agent' in their answer — this is the client's own language and does NOT fail the gate. Only scan AI-authored question/prose text.

terms.approvedReplacement: (internal use only — never surface to clients)
terms.rationale: Methodology name that sounds impersonal and corporate

terms.approvedReplacement: (internal use only — never surface to clients)
terms.rationale: Lean Six Sigma phase acronym that has no meaning to business owners

notes: The system uses Lean Six Sigma methodology INTERNALLY (standard work templates, quality gates, value-stream handoffs, continuous improvement) — but the owner never hears those words.
This file is the ONLY canonical source for this list. INSTRUCTIONS.md and build-workforce.py contain pointers to this file, not a second copy.
Scanner must use word-boundary matching (\b) and case-insensitive comparison.
Multi-word terms (e.g. 'tech stack', 'sub-agent') must match as complete phrases.
See clientAnswerExempt field on individual terms — some terms are only forbidden in AI-authored text, not in quoted client answers.
